authoritative_sources.title: The NIST Definition of Cloud Computing (SP 800-145)
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines cloud deployment/service model language needed to separate tenant and provider responsibilities in data center compliance workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Cloud Computing Synopsis and Recommendations (SP 800-146)
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides vendor-neutral cloud deployment and management context used to normalize cloud operations obligations and controls.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Special Publication 800-53, Revision 5
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary control catalog for security, access, logging, and evidence disciplines used across data center and cloud compliance tasks.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Contingency Planning Guide for Federal Information Systems and Organizations (SP 800-34 Rev. 1)
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines continuity planning and recovery evidence expectations that directly inform exception and attestation stages.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Special Publication 800-137, Information Security Continuous Monitoring
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative model for continuous evidence refresh and monitoring controls in operations-heavy compliance workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 27017:2015 Cloud Security
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry standard for cloud security controls and role boundaries, directly relevant to shared control interpretation.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 27018:2019 Protection of PII in Public Clouds
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides data processing and privacy control expectations in cloud environments when PII-linked tenant obligations appear in scope.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 22301:2019 Business continuity management systems
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary standard for continuity readiness and evidence expectations in service and facility operations.

authoritative_sources.title: ASHRAE Thermal Guidelines for Data Centers
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines data center environmental operating expectations that affect facility compliance artifacts and operational evidence quality.

authoritative_sources.title: Cloud Controls Matrix (CCM)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry framework for cloud control catalog, control terminology, and governance mapping used for consistent cross-vendor interpretation.

authoritative_sources.title: NAICS 518210 Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Industry terminology and record normalization source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Confirms industry-class context and terminology baseline required to distinguish this lane from generic cross-industry compliance.
used_for: failure-mode detection when names are ambiguous

workflow_stages.name: Requirement and policy intake
workflow_stages.description: Resolve request intent, tenancy context, obligations, and source-of-truth precedence before any compliance recommendation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Control and evidence mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map obligations to control evidence, owner chains, and retrieval sources; identify missing evidence and unresolved dependencies.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Industry terminology and record normalization source

workflow_stages.name: Exception, remediation, and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Convert unresolved obligations into escalation and remediation routing with clear owners, blockers, and assumptions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Review and attestation preparation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare attestation-ready summaries only when evidence ownership, policy context, and authority precedence are explicit.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and conflict resolution
workflow_stages.description: Return orchestration-safe outcomes for legal/finality requests, conflicts, or missing context before any completion statement.
source_classes: Industry terminology and record normalization source
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Data center floor/zone mappings and DCIM records
systems_of_record: Cloud control plane and tenant provisioning metadata

allowed: Map obligations to tenant and public-standard control expectations in evidence-bound outputs.
Prepare compliant exception and remediation packets for missing records.
Escalate policy conflict and missing context to adjacent owners without overstating authority.
escalate: Policy conflicts between public guidance and tenant policy that alter outcome
Missing source-of-truth systems or unknown ownership chains
Conflicting continuity or attestation interpretations that cannot be resolved at lane scope
refuse: Requests for legal opinion, regulator-facing final language, or formal signoff
Requests for engineering/safety change authority, financial approval, or direct operations control
Claims of completion where evidence or owner confirmation is incomplete

domain_failure_modes: Returning completion despite missing IAM, change, or control-evidence artifacts.
Treating cloud public guidance as superior to explicit tenant policy, contract, or regulatory precedence.
Claiming final legal/regulatory position without adjacent approval authority.
Collapsing data-center facility and cloud tenancy terminology and losing material scope boundaries.
Proceeding with attestation content while attestation owner matrix is unresolved.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether any tenant has stricter local regulator obligations beyond public standards
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: When local regulator obligations exist, the lane must return cannot-decide and escalate to tenant approver before completion.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant policy precedence for cloud-shared responsibility terms
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: No default is assumed. The tenant control dictionary and approved authority map must be requested before confident final mapping.

notes: Source set combines cloud, security-control, continuity, and data-center facility references to cover workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; unresolved points are converted into explicit orchestration return conditions and escalation requirements.
Industry mapping is materially differentiated through shared-control interpretation, tenancy terminology, and facility-dependent evidence requirements.
